title: SolAmerica Energy Finalizes $100 million Revolving Credit Facility with Deutsche Bank to Fund Portfolio of Solar Generation Assets
content: SolAmerica Energy (SAE), an Atlanta-based developer of distributed solar projects owned by AB CarVal funds, has closed a $100 million revolving credit facility with Deutsche Bank to finance project development, equipment purchases, and construction across the U.S. Southeast, East Coast, and Midwest. The deal, Deutsche Bank acting as Administrative and Collateral Agent, marks SAEâ€™s third financing since its 2023 acquisition by AB CarVal, following a $50 million construction loan with KeyBank and an $80 million+ tax equity transaction with Key Equipment Finance. Executives from SAE, Deutsche Bank, and AB CarVal highlighted the facility as a sign of strong lender confidence and a step toward scaling affordable, reliable renewable energy to meet rising U.S. demand.
author: Analysts Account
